message:
fix(pthread): reject negative tv_sec in cond_timedwait (bd-dc9mc)

futex_wait_timed computed `deadline.tv_sec - now.tv_sec` with an
unchecked i64 subtraction. A caller passing a timespec with tv_sec
near i64::MIN (e.g. a negative absolute deadline) crashed the
process in debug builds ("attempt to subtract with overflow") and
wrapped to a ~9.2e18-second effectively-infinite timeout in release
builds — losing the ETIMEDOUT contract on every pthread_cond_timedwait
/ _clockwait / _timedlock path.

Fix: tighten absolute_timespec_valid to require tv_sec >= 0, and use
i64::checked_sub on the deadline arithmetic so any remaining extreme
values map to ETIMEDOUT instead of panicking or wrapping.

Two regression tests pin the new contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
